analysis: Alpha estimation noise vs observed variation
conclusion: Observed CV (2.28%) is within 1.09x of expected estimation noise (2.08%). Per-model alpha variation is entirely consistent with regression estimation noise, confirming true universality of alpha across architectures. The r=-0.55 FAIL of per-model rho predicting per-model alpha is expected because there is no true per-model alpha signal to predict.
implication: The centroid-overlap dispersion experiment is not needed: there is no unexplained per-model alpha variance to capture. Alpha is a universal constant.
